name: Texas City Failure Timeline (Compressed)
description: Replays the BP Texas City overfill signature in compressed time. The vessel is genuinely filling (net inflow positive) but the level transmitter freezes — indicated level stays flat while the true level keeps rising. Physics (the flow-implied inventory) disagrees with the frozen reading, so the level is quarantined and the trusted flow-implied level becomes the substitute. A decoupled valve, a drifting temperature, and a later-frozen pressure transmitter add realistic accompanying failures.
